3rd-strata/hytropo.html.title: Hytropo
3rd-strata/hytropo.html.description:
The realm is named Hytropo. (Hi-trope-oh)Surrounding the Engine is a maelstrom of energy, a mere 9 meter gap between the surface of the engine to the surrounding stone. The space between blasted by a storm of mana, wind, heat, and souls.Then, there are kilometers of dug out caverns and tunnels, forming brood chambers, sleep chambers, choke points, and other structures within the hive of the slivers.The tunnels lose their number and become more natural as you move outwards, more akin to cracks within a stone than true tunnels. These are all within a kilometers thick pillar of black stone that rises in the central 350.1 km radius of the realm, going all the way up until the exosphere.360 km band surrounding this pillar is the neutral territory. A flat landscape of stone broken up by rivers that flow across the expanse, and a light persistent white fog, is what one would see looking out onto the land. A thin sheet of light blue lichen imitates grasses upon the stone, the rivers are covered in a patina of black algae, and the fog are fungal spores. If you or I went into this environment, we would choke on the spores or they would take root within our lungs overwhelming our immune systems. If that didn't kill us, the algae bloom in the water would poison us.The rest of the realm is the Exosphere.The great sky wurm is the only notable native fauna. Flying through the sky, it rampages across the realm when it spots potential prey. They fly through the sky and never seem to truly land, though, because of this, it seems that there are little more than a dozen of these beings within the realm.
The only consistent Eldrazi incursions are those of the Warden of Geometries.
Gremlins scavenge off of the Kin, Splinters, and Dreamers; going after any metalworks they have made. Because of the spores, their equipment often fails and are sometimes left behind to 'appease the gremlins'.Pests riffle through cracks in the stone for worms and munch on fungal grasses. Some will grow to prestigious sizes, and are the main food source for the sky wurms. Some pests grow fungal masses on their backs, some as a symbiosis, others as parasitism.
The Kin of Hytropo are beings made of light blue goo that connects chitinous plates together, faerie-like wings connected to individual plates extend from their backs. Though, due to the sky wurms, it is unlikely to see one of them flying in the sky. They have insectile kin legs with chitinous human-like arms. Their heads have two large eye-like globs on the sides of their heads and two pairs of smaller eye-like globs. They don't have any hair to speak of.The Kin eek out an existence passively subsisting on fungal spores that touches their gooey flesh, though they will occasionally hunt a gremlin or pest when necessary. In the past fifty or so years, they have begun to mine etherium and moxite to trade with the Dreamers.
The Splinters carapace are grey, marbled by with white, blue, and black and their eyes are a grey blue. Splinters will often hug the outside of the central spire.Terrain; Cloudpost, Glimmerpost, Trenchpost L^2 Planar Nexus 4 / Lsn Sliver Hive s Raffine's Tower n Contaminated Landscape 2n Glimmervoid n^1 Mana Confluence n^2 Echoes of Eternity n^3 Hive; Thrumming Hivepool triangular(n) Riptide Island sqrt(n) Crawlspace s Non-Legendary Slivers n Animals; Gremlins Fungi Pests Worms Chromium
Eldrazi Sapients; Splinter Kin - Faerie, Insect, Germ Dreamer

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
